  • back.core.graphdb – Pluggable Graph Database Backends
  • back.core.graphql – GraphQL Schema Generation
  • back.core.industry – Industry Standard Ontology Importers
  • back.core.reasoning – OWL 2 RL & SWRL Inference
  • back.objects – Application domain objects
  • back.core.sqlwizard – LLM-Assisted SQL Generation
  • back.core.triplestore – Pluggable RDF/Graph Backends
  • back.core.w3c – W3C Standard Parsers & Generators

front.routes & internal JSON API – Web UI and HTMX/API helpers¶

Frontend HTML route – Registry page.

async front.routes.registry.registry_page(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Registry management page – browse projects, configure registry location.

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

Frontend HTML route – Entity URI resolution.

Accepts an ontology entity URI (path-based or query-param) and redirects to the Knowledge Graph visualization with the entity focused.

When no explicit domain query-parameter is supplied the route inspects the URI against all registry domains’ base URIs and automatically selects the owning domain so the Knowledge Graph page can load the correct graph.

Cross-domain bridges are handled server-side: the target domain is loaded into the session before the redirect, so the browser only needs a single page load to display the graph.

async front.routes.resolve.resolve_entity_query(request, uri=Query(None), domain=Query(None), session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Resolve an entity URI passed as a query parameter.

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • uri (str)

  • domain (str | None)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

async front.routes.resolve.resolve_entity_path(request, uri, domain=Query(None), session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Resolve an entity URI embedded in the URL path.

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • uri (str)

  • domain (str | None)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

Internal JSON API routers¶

Internal API – Home / session / validation JSON endpoints.

Moved from app/frontend/home/routes.py during the front/back split.

async api.routers.internal.home.navbar_state(session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Consolidated navbar state: domain info, validation, dtwin cache, warehouse.

Returns everything the navbar needs in a single round-trip so the client avoids issuing four or five separate requests on every page load.

Parameters:

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

Save Databricks configuration.

Host/token are per-session. Warehouse ID is instance-global (admin only). Catalog/schema are NOT stored – they are selected dynamically when needed.

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

async api.routers.internal.settings.select_warehouse(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Select a SQL warehouse.

Tries to persist the choice globally (UC Volume) so all users share it. When the registry is not configured yet (bootstrap scenario), falls back to storing in the session so the user can immediately browse catalogs and set up the registry.

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

List users and groups from the Databricks App permissions for the picker.

Always fetches fresh data (bypasses cache) so newly added app users appear immediately in the dropdown.

Parameters:

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

async api.routers.internal.settings.search_principals(q='', type='user', session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Search all workspace users or groups via SCIM.

Query parameter q is the search term (min 2 chars). Query parameter type is user or group.

Parameters:

  • type (str)

  • settings (Settings)

Create or update an entity group.

Body: { "group": { "name", "label", "description", "color", "icon", "members": [...] }, "index": -1 } When index >= 0 the group at that position is replaced; otherwise a new group is appended (duplicating a name is prevented).

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

async api.routers.internal.ontology.get_wizard_templates()[source]¶

Return the predefined wizard quick-templates.

async api.routers.internal.ontology.generate_ontology_async(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Start ontology generation via agent_owl_generator (background task).

Poll GET /tasks/{task_id} for owl_content, stats, and agent trace fields. There is no synchronous generate endpoint; this is the only LLM wizard entry point.

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

async api.routers.internal.ontology.auto_assign_icons(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Use the auto-icon-assign agent to suggest emoji icons for entity names.

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

async api.routers.internal.ontology.ontology_assistant_chat(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Process a single chat turn with the ontology assistant agent.

Expects JSON body:
{

“message”: “Remove the entity Customer”, “history”: […] // optional prior conversation messages

}

Returns:

{

“success”: true/false, “reply”: “…”, “ontology_changed”: true/false, “config”: { classes, properties, … } // returned when ontology was modified

}

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

async api.routers.internal.ontology.ontology_assistant_invoke(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Invoke the Ontology Assistant via the MLflow ResponsesAgent interface.

Accepts the OpenAI Responses-compatible schema used by the Databricks Agent Framework. The caller can either supply Databricks credentials in custom_inputs or let the route fill them from the active session.

Expects JSON body (ResponsesAgentRequest):

{
    "input": [
        {"role": "user", "content": "Add an entity called Vehicle"}
    ],
    "custom_inputs": {          // optional overrides
        "host": "...",
        "token": "...",
        "endpoint_name": "..."
    }
}

Returns a ResponsesAgentResponse with custom_outputs containing the mutated ontology when changes were made.

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

async api.routers.internal.mapping.toggle_exclude(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Toggle the excluded flag on individual mapping entries.

The excluded boolean is stored directly on each entry in entities (keyed by ontology_class) or relationships (keyed by property). If no entry exists yet for an excluded item a minimal stub is created.

Body JSON:

uris: list[str] – URIs to toggle excluded: bool – True to exclude, False to include item_type: str – ‘entity’ or ‘relationship’ (default ‘entity’)

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

async api.routers.internal.mapping.get_schema_context(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

async api.routers.internal.mapping.generate_sql_from_prompt(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Generate SQL from natural language prompt using LLM.

Request body:

endpoint_name: Name of the model serving endpoint catalog: Unity Catalog catalog name schema: Schema name prompt: Natural language description of the query limit: Optional result limit (default: 100) validate_plan: Whether to run EXPLAIN validation (default: true) schema_context: Optional pre-built schema context with tables (from domain metadata) mapping_type: Type of mapping (‘entity’, ‘relationship’, or None for general)

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

async api.routers.internal.mapping.validate_sql(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Request body:

sql: SQL query to validate catalog: Unity Catalog catalog name schema: Schema name validate_plan: Whether to run EXPLAIN validation (default: false)

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

Start the auto-mapping agent for a single entity or relationship.

Returns a task_id immediately; the caller polls /tasks/{task_id} for the result. Uses the same agent as the batch auto-map but scoped to one item.

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

async api.routers.internal.dtwin.get_groups(session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Return ontology entity groups for the Sigma graph expand/collapse feature.

Each group contains the member class names so the frontend can build super-nodes for collapsed groups and restore member nodes on expand.

Parameters:

session_mgr (SessionManager)

async api.routers.internal.dtwin.start_triplestore_sync(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Start async dual digital twin build: CREATE VIEW (Triple-Store) then populate LadybugDB graph.

Supports two modes controlled by the build_mode body parameter:

  • "incremental" (default) — version-gate check + server-side diff via a Delta snapshot table. Falls back to full when no snapshot exists or when the diff exceeds a threshold.

  • "full" — drop and recreate the graph (legacy behaviour).

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

async api.routers.internal.dtwin.load_triplestore(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Load triples from the graph database and return them as query results.

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

async api.routers.internal.dtwin.detect_clusters(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Run community detection on the full knowledge graph.

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

async api.routers.internal.dtwin.filter_triplestore(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Query the triple store with filter criteria and return only matching triples.

Supports two phases via the phase field:

  • "preview" (default) — run seed search only and return a flat list of matching entities with their type and label so the user can pick which ones to explore.

  • "expand" — accept selected_uris (list of subject URIs chosen by the user in the preview modal) and run the depth expansion + triple fetch.

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

async api.routers.internal.dtwin.triplestore_changes(session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Check if ontology or assignments changed since the last build.

Parameters:

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

async api.routers.internal.dtwin.triplestore_status(session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None), refresh=False)[source]¶

Lightweight check: does the triple store table exist and contain data?

Returns session-cached status when available; falls back to a live query and caches the result. refresh is accepted for API compatibility and ignored.

Parameters:

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

  • refresh (bool)

async api.routers.internal.dtwin.sync_info(session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Return all data the Digital Twin Information page needs in one shot.

Graph status and artefact existence are served from the session cache when available (populated after each successful build). On a cache miss the values are fetched live from Databricks and then cached for the next request.

Parameters:

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

async api.routers.internal.dtwin.dt_existence(session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Check existence of each Digital Twin artefact.

Returns session-cached results when available; falls back to live Databricks checks and caches the result.

Parameters:

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

async api.routers.internal.dtwin.reload_graph_from_registry(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Download the LadybugDB archive from the registry volume and extract it locally.

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

async api.routers.internal.dtwin.drop_snapshot(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Drop the incremental snapshot table to force a full rebuild on next build.

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

async api.routers.internal.dtwin.triplestore_stats(session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None), refresh=False)[source]¶

Return content statistics about the triple store.

Parameters:

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

  • refresh (bool)

async api.routers.internal.dtwin.execute_dataquality_check(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

async api.routers.internal.dtwin.execute_quality_check(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Execute a quality check against the triple store table.

Deprecated since version Use: /dataquality/* endpoints instead (SHACL-driven data quality).

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

Start all quality checks as an asynchronous task.

  • settings (Settings)

Internal API – Domain management JSON endpoints.

Moved from app/frontend/project/routes.py during the front/back split.

async api.routers.internal.domain.get_domain_info(session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Get current domain information.

Parameters:

session_mgr (SessionManager)

async api.routers.internal.domain.save_domain_info(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Save domain information.

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

async api.routers.internal.domain.get_current_user(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Return the current user’s display name and email.

In Databricks App mode the proxy headers carry the real user identity. Falls back to the SCIM /Me endpoint for local / PAT mode.

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

  • settings (Settings)

Save domain to session (for export).

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

async api.routers.internal.domain.export_domain(session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Export complete domain as JSON.

Note: Generated outputs (R2RML, OWL) are NOT exported - they are regenerated from source data.

Parameters:

session_mgr (SessionManager)

async api.routers.internal.domain.import_domain(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Import domain from JSON (supports both file upload and JSON body).

For versioned domains, pass ‘version’ parameter to load a specific version.

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

async api.routers.internal.domain.reset_domain(session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Reset entire domain to empty state.

Parameters:

session_mgr (SessionManager)

async api.routers.internal.domain.clear_domain(session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Clear current domain and start fresh (delegates to reset).

Parameters:

session_mgr (SessionManager)

async api.routers.internal.domain.get_session_debug(session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Get full session data for debugging purposes.

Only available when LOG_LEVEL is set to DEBUG.

Parameters:

session_mgr (SessionManager)

async api.routers.internal.domain.get_app_debug()[source]¶

Expose global in-memory caches for debugging.

Only available when LOG_LEVEL is set to DEBUG.

async api.routers.internal.domain.get_domain_config(session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Get domain-specific configuration.

The warehouse_id is read-only here (set globally via Settings by admins). Catalog/schema are NOT stored – they are selected dynamically when needed.

Parameters:

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

async api.routers.internal.domain.save_domain_config(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Save domain-specific configuration.

Note: warehouse_id is no longer stored per-project (it is instance-global). Catalog/schema are NOT stored – they are selected dynamically when needed.

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

Parameters:

  • settings (Settings)

async api.routers.internal.domain.create_new_version(session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

  • settings (Settings)

Get current version status and fetch available versions from registry.

Results are cached server-side for a short TTL. Pass ?refresh=true to force a fresh UC lookup.

Parameters:

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

  • refresh (bool)

async api.routers.internal.domain.list_version_details(session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

List all versions with per-version description, mcp_enabled flag, and status.

Parameters:

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

async api.routers.internal.domain.set_version_mcp(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

async api.routers.internal.domain.get_metadata(session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Get stored Unity Catalog metadata from session.

Parameters:

session_mgr (SessionManager)

async api.routers.internal.domain.list_schema_tables(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

List all tables in a schema without loading full metadata.

Returns table names only for selection before loading.

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

async api.routers.internal.domain.initialize_metadata(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Load Unity Catalog metadata by reading tables and columns from a schema.

This merges new tables with existing metadata - existing tables are preserved, only new tables are added. Table and column comments are fetched from UC.

If ‘selected_tables’ is provided, only those tables will be loaded.

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

async api.routers.internal.domain.save_metadata(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Save selected tables to metadata.

This allows users to filter which tables are kept in the metadata. Tables should have full_name field (catalog.schema.table). For backwards compatibility, if full_name is missing, it will be constructed from existing metadata’s catalog/schema or legacy fields.

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

async api.routers.internal.domain.clear_metadata(session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Clear stored Unity Catalog metadata from session.

Parameters:

session_mgr (SessionManager)

async api.routers.internal.domain.update_table_location(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Update the data-source location (catalog.schema) for metadata tables.

Rewrites the table’s full_name to catalog.schema.table_name. When apply_all is true every table in the metadata is updated.

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

async api.routers.internal.domain.update_mappings_from_metadata(session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Push catalog/schema from metadata tables into entity and relationship mappings.

Reads each metadata table’s full_name, extracts catalog and schema, and updates matching entity mappings (catalog, schema fields) and relationship mappings (source_table, target_table fields).

Parameters:

session_mgr (SessionManager)

async api.routers.internal.domain.initialize_metadata_async(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Start async metadata loading and return task ID.

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

async api.routers.internal.domain.update_metadata_async(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Start async metadata update and return task ID.

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

async api.routers.internal.domain.update_metadata(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Update metadata for already loaded tables by re-fetching from Unity Catalog.

This refreshes column information for existing tables while preserving user-edited comments/descriptions. New columns are added, removed columns are deleted.

If ‘table_names’ is provided, only those tables will be updated.

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

async api.routers.internal.domain.list_documents(session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

List files in the domain volume’s documents directory.

Parameters:

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

async api.routers.internal.domain.upload_documents(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Upload one or more files to the domain volume’s documents directory.

Accepts multipart/form-data with field name files.

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

async api.routers.internal.domain.delete_document(request, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Delete a file from the domain volume’s documents directory.

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

async api.routers.internal.domain.preview_document(filename, session_mgr=Depends(dependency=<function get_session_manager>, use_cache=True, scope=None), settings=Depends(dependency=<functools._lru_cache_wrapper object>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Stream a document from the domain volume for in-browser preview.

Binary files (PDF, images) are streamed with the appropriate content-type. Text files are returned as JSON with a content field.

Parameters:

  • filename (str)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

Internal API – Task management JSON endpoints.

Moved from app/frontend/tasks/routes.py during the front/back split.

async api.routers.internal.tasks.list_tasks(include_completed=True)[source]¶

List all tasks.

Parameters:

include_completed (bool)

async api.routers.internal.tasks.get_task(task_id)[source]¶

Get a specific task by ID.

Parameters:

task_id (str)

async api.routers.internal.tasks.cancel_task(task_id)[source]¶

Cancel a running task.

Parameters:

task_id (str)

async api.routers.internal.tasks.clear_completed_tasks()[source]¶

Clear all completed/failed/cancelled tasks.

Services¶

Home service for validation and status operations.

Pure business logic functions for home page operations. Session management is handled by FastAPI routes.

back.services.home.get_session_status(domain)[source]¶

Get current session status for navbar indicators.

Delegates to DomainSession.get_session_status().

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

back.services.home.validate_status(domain)[source]¶

Get validation status for navbar indicators (computed on each call).

Parameters:

domain – DomainSession instance

Returns:

Complete validation status

Return type:

dict

back.services.home.validate_ontology(domain)[source]¶

Validate current ontology.

Parameters:

domain – DomainSession instance

Returns:

Validation result with errors

Return type:

dict

async back.services.home.get_detailed_validation(domain, settings, warehouse_id='')[source]¶

Get detailed validation status.

Digital Twin graph status and artefact existence are served from the session cache when available (populated after each successful build). On a cache miss the values are fetched live and then cached.

Parameters:

  • domain – DomainSession instance

  • settings – App settings (Databricks / warehouse resolution)

  • warehouse_id (str) – resolved warehouse ID (empty string if none)

Returns:

Comprehensive validation details

Return type:

dict

async back.services.home.get_navbar_state(domain, settings, warehouse_id='')[source]¶

Build the navbar state in a single call.

The navbar only displays domain identity and SQL Warehouse status, so this intentionally skips the expensive Digital Twin and validation I/O that used to run here. Those checks are available on the validation page (/validate/detailed) and the DT Sync page (/dtwin/sync/info).

Parameters:

warehouse_id (str)

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

Configuration business logic for Databricks settings, registry, permissions, and related UI flows.

HTTP routing stays in routes; this module holds orchestration, validation, and data shaping.

back.services.settings.is_warehouse_locked(settings)[source]¶

True when the SQL Warehouse is supplied by a Databricks App resource.

Parameters:

settings (Settings)

Return type:

bool

back.services.settings.is_registry_locked(settings)[source]¶

True when the registry is supplied by a Databricks App Volume resource.

Parameters:

settings (Settings)

Return type:

bool

back.services.settings.require_admin_error(request, session_mgr, settings)[source]¶

Raise AuthorizationError if the caller is not an admin in Databricks App mode.

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

Dict[str, Any]

back.services.settings.set_registry_version_active_result(domain_name, version, enabled, session_mgr, settings)[source]¶

Toggle the active (mcp_enabled) flag for a specific version.

Works on any domain in the registry — the domain does not need to be loaded in the current session. Only one version per domain may be active; enabling one automatically disables the others.

Parameters:

  • domain_name (str)

  • version (str)

  • enabled (bool)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

back.services.settings.search_workspace_principals(query, principal_type, session_mgr, settings)[source]¶

Search users or groups that have access to the Databricks App.

Fetches the full app-permission principal list (cached by PermissionService) and applies a case-insensitive contains filter on the client side. This avoids SCIM calls that the app service-principal typically cannot perform and ensures only app-visible principals are returned.

Parameters:

  • query (str)

  • principal_type (str)

  • session_mgr (SessionManager)

  • settings (Settings)

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]
